what_it_does: Records metadata for a design-source image asset named p-05 in the presentation-15p set and labels it as design ground-truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id, address image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-05, channel design-source, path, blob reference, mime image/jpeg, name p-05, width and height both 0, source design_source_material, author_session ch-3mpkjg3r, created timestamp, and empty links array.
Body text reads 'SOURCE design ground-truth — presentation-15p/p-05.jpg'.
No code symbols are present; the file is a ledger stub for an image.
standouts: Width and height are both 0 despite being an image
Explicitly tagged as SOURCE design ground-truth
concerns: Image dimensions w and h are 0, which may indicate missing or uncomputed metadata.
links array is empty, so no explicit relationships to other assets are recorded.
notes: A ledger stub for a single JPEG slide asset from a 15-page presentation, marked as design ground-truth.
questions: Why are width and height zero for an image file?
What does the blob hash correspond to and is it verifiable?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to present design-source material; the file only stores metadata and a ground-truth label, not the image content itself, so it acts as a pointer/ledger entry rather than the asset.
intention_signals: To register and identify a specific presentation slide image as an authoritative design-source artifact within the channel memory ledger.
contribution: Provides a stable, content-addressed metadata record that links a presentation slide image to its design-source channel and ground-truth status.
summary_for_embedding: Metadata ledger entry for the JPEG image p-05 in the presentation-15p design-source channel, tagged as SOURCE design ground-truth with a blob reference.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a single presentation slide image as an authoritative design-source artifact for later reference and fusion.
